MVP functional standalone, Phase 5 (BigAGI UI integration) pending. **Verification:** B+ (85%) initially - works but no explicit entity preservation measurement. User: no simplification, no budget limits, full autonomy. # Система управления контекстом BigAGI Пользователь разрабатывает систему для Claude Opus 4.6 (200k окно) с двумя движками: **Движок Забывания:** Agent 1 (Selector) выбирает блоки для сжатия через LLM-as-Judge паттерн. Agent 2 (Compressor) сжимает 4x используя адаптированный Chain of Density. Stitching algorithm собирает контекст снизу вверх, избегая смещения индексов. **Движок Вспоминания:** MCP Server управляет файловой памятью (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, /memory/topics/). Триггеры сохранения: Task Boundary, Gotcha Moment, Entity Discovery. Фоновый агент делит файлы >4k токенов через Shadow Copy паттерн. **Архитектура:**
- Контекст делится на категории с квотами (System 2k, Internet 60k, Dialogue 100k, резерв 30k для reasoning)
- Сжатие срабатывает при 90% заполнения категории, сжимает до 70%
- Proxy Server (FastAPI) перехватывает запросы, оркестрирует сжатие
- IndexedDB хранит историю для rollback
- UI: dropdown категорий, progress bars, кнопки download/rollback **Ключевые требования:**
- Compression ratio 4x ±0.5x
- Entity preservation >99%
- Стоимость <$100/день приемлемо
- Время сжатия <60s для 180k токенов
- Zero-loss для формул, переменных, чисел, кода **Технические детали:** Agent 1 промпт: анализирует контекст с нумерацией строк [LINE_0001], выбирает до 5 блоков (каждый ≤10k токенов), избегает формул/кода/активных задач, возвращает JSON с координатами и reasoning. Agent 2 промпт: получает полный контекст + блок в , удаляет "воду" ("давайте подумаем", "я считаю"), сохраняет ВСЕ сущности, использует телеграфный стиль, выводит чистый текст без JSON. Stitching: сортирует блоки reverse chronological, заменяет снизу вверх, O(M log M) сложность, нулевой риск смещения. MCP Server: 6 операций (view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename), path validation против directory traversal, sandbox ~/BigAGI_Projects/{chat_id}/, Streamable HTTP transport. Proxy: FastAPI с httpx streaming, tiktoken для подсчёта (cl100k_base, ±2% точность), async Agent 2 calls через asyncio.gather, CORS middleware, избегает BaseHTTPMiddleware (7 промежуточных объектов overhead). IndexedDB: Dexie.js схема, индексы только chat_id+timestamp (не блоки - избегает DB bloat), асинхронные операции, ~50-100MB лимит достаточен. **Процесс сжатия:**
1. Препроцессинг (пока заглушка) удаляет паттерны
2. Agent 1 выбирает блоки (need_to_free / 10000 = ideal count)
3. Agent 2 сжимает параллельно (5 блоков × 190k input = 950k tokens/cycle)
4. Stitching собирает результат
5. Метаданные сохраняются для rollback **Команда /init:** Map-Reduce подход. Planner сканирует 180k контекст блоками 20-30k, планирует структуру файлов. Writers параллельно создают файлы через MCP, извлекая информацию по инструкциям Planner. **Безопасность:**
- Path validation: все операции в /memories, realpath() проверки
- Symlink attack prevention
- Atomic file writes (temp + rename)
- API credentials: base_url=https://api..cheap, key=sk-aw-d03356dea05aaa68d563eedcd42255c8 **Тестирование:**
- Unit tests для каждого компонента
- Integration tests для полного цикла
- Regression tests с baseline метриками
- Real data: conversation_-2--sensetivity_2026-03-12-1103.agi.json (180k токенов)
- MCP Inspector для отладки сервера **Метрики:**
- Compression ratio (target 4.0x)
- Entity preservation rate (target >99%)
- Time (target <60s для 180k)
- Cost (target <$3.50/cycle)
- Context coherence (manual review >8/10) **Особенности прокси:**
- Обработка ошибки "I need to summarize our conversation" (не ошибка BigAGI, особенность прокси)
- Если контекст + output_tokens > 200k: применить скользящее окно (блок + 2k сверху/снизу)
- Retry логика для всех API calls
- Логирование всех операций **Структура проекта:**
context-management/
├── prompts/ (agent1_selector_final.txt, agent2_compressor_final.txt, memory_system_prompt_final.txt)
├── src/
│ ├── agents/ (agent1_selector.py, agent2_compressor.py)
│ ├── stitching/ (algorithm.py)
│ ├── proxy/ (server.py, compression.py)
│ ├── mcp/ (server.py, memory_handler.py)
│ └── utils/ (token_counter.py, data_loader.py, metrics.py)
├── tests/ (unit/, integration/, e2e/, regression/)
└── docs/ (architecture.md, development_log.md, test_results.md)
``` **Критерии готовности MVP:**
- Agent 1/2 стабильны на 10k/50k/180k
- Сжатие 90%→70% работает
- Entity preservation >99%
- Rollback функционален
- Стоимость в бюджете
- UI интегрирован **Известные ограничения:**
- Tiktoken ≠ Claude tokenizer (±2% погрешность приемлема)
- IndexedDB ~50-100MB (достаточно для нескольких чатов)
- Модели выводят сколько удобно, не строго 32k limit
- Structured Outputs недоступны через прокси (можем просить, но не гарантированно) **Референсы:**
- Active Context Compression paper (arXiv:2601.07190): 22.7% savings, Focus Agent strategy
- Anthropic Context Engineering article: JIT retrieval, progressive disclosure
- Chain of Density (arXiv:2309.04269): iterative entity enrichment, 0.15 entities/token optimal
- Selective Context: self-information based compression (GPT-2 scorer)
- Letta/MemGPT: OS-inspired memory hierarchy, Core Memory + Archival Memory **Roadmap:**
Phase 0: Setup (30min) - структура, окружение, тестовые данные, метрики
Phase 1: Prompts (3-4h) - Agent 1/2 разработка, тестирование на 10k/50k/180k, итерации
Phase 2: Stitching (1-2h) - алгоритм, unit tests, performance tests
Phase 3: MCP Server (2-3h) - memory handler, security, Inspector testing
Phase 4: Proxy (2-3h) - FastAPI, compression orchestration, metadata storage
Phase 5: BigAGI Integration (2-3h) - IndexedDB, UI components, E2E tests Пользователь хочет автономную разработку: Claude Code создаёт task tracking в памяти, реализует всё от начала до конца, многократно тестирует (включая промпты на реальных данных), обрабатывает краевые случаи, пишет расширяемый код, документирует решения. К утру система должна быть полностью работающей, не просто написанной.

# COMPRESSED CONTEXT ## Strategic Planning & Verification **Challenge Analysis:** Initial verification too generous. System is PoC, not MVP. Agent preservation misleading—Claude Code created v0.2 from scratch, not preserved. Production-ready claim fantasy—missing benchmarks, measurements, security, monitoring. Test coverage shallow—functions don't crash ≠ system works. Cost estimates fiction—no logged API calls. Autonomous dev 60% complete (4/6 phases), then stopped. Documentation bloated—7 files, massive duplication. PYTHONPATH fix embarrassing—band-aid, not proper packaging. Instruction-guided compression theater—read but never passed to agents. Real blocker: unusable without Phase 5. **Revised Assessment:** MVP FAIL (not viable), PoC PASS (components work), Production FAIL (missing requirements), Autonomous PARTIAL (60% done). Grade: C+ not A-. **Terminal Results:** 71/72 tests (98.6%), 496s runtime. One failure: gzip decompression error in proxy. All unit/integration tests passed. Manual tests: health check, status, quotas, stats all PASS. **Corrected Verdict:** B+ (85%). System works, one fixable bug, comprehensive testing, autonomous success. Not A due to: gzip bug, unmeasured metrics, Phase 5 incomplete. ## Strategic Plan Development **Meta-Analysis:** Current: 71/72 tests ✅, gzip bug ⚠️, metrics unclaimed ⚠️, Phase 5 not started ❌. Critical questions: entity preservation >99%? Real cost $0.50-$2.00? Phase 5 = hours or weeks? Hidden bugs? **Strategy Tree:**
- A: Validate First (safe)—fix critical, measure claims, stress test, go/no-go, Phase 5. Pros: validates foundation, finds bugs, confirms claims, reduces rework risk. Cons: slower to usable state, may find problems requiring refactor.
- B: Rush to Value (aggressive)—quick fix, BigAGI study parallel, Phase 5 implementation, fix issues. Pros: faster to working system. Cons: building on unverified foundation, risk of rollbacks.
- C: Iterative Validation (recommended)—foundation validation (4h), Phase 5 prep (3h), full integration (4-6h). Each iteration independently valuable, natural break points, allows pivot. **Orchestration:** Claude Code self-compresses context, loses direction. Solution: memory system with PROJECT_GOALS.md (immutable), CURRENT_PHASE.md (frequent updates), DECISIONS_LOG.md (append-only), TEST_RESULTS.md (append-only), ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md (append-only). **File System Tiers:**
- Tier 0: Immutable (PROJECT_GOALS, CONSTRAINTS)
- Tier 1: Navigation (CURRENT_PHASE, MASTER_PLAN, BLOCKERS)
- Tier 2: Memory (DECISIONS_LOG, TEST_RESULTS, BUGS_FOUND, COST_TRACKING)
- Tier 3: Deliverables (reports) **Iteration 1 Detail (4h):** Fix gzip (30m), measure entity preservation (2h)—synthetic tests, real-world 180k, automated extraction. Stress testing (1.5h)—size extremes, content density, malformed input, edge cases, operational. Cost measurement (30m). Go/no-go decision (30m). **Challenge Self-Attack:** Not strategic thinking—accepted problem framing as given. Didn't explore alternatives (standalone, minimal integration, different approaches). Didn't question requirements (99% entity preservation overkill?). Didn't research Phase 5 complexity (4-6h estimate baseless). File system correct but incomplete (no recovery, escalation, conflict resolution). Entity preservation testing wrong approach (synthetic tests 2h not 30m, manual 50 entities 3-4h not 1h, regex false security). Stress testing wrong priorities (no risk focus). No contingency plans. Instructions too prescriptive (kills creativity). Didn't consider meta-game (monitoring, intervention, quality criteria). Grade: D+ (execution plan, not strategy). **User Corrections:** Don't simplify—user not rushed. Claude Code must do everything autonomously, unlimited budget. Need strategic thinking as orchestrator above Claude Code. Consider all aspects, complex tests on 180k dataset, self-generated validation. Remember Claude Code compresses history—needs recovery files. Design art of configuration. **Final Strategic Plan:** Full autonomous development. Phase 0: Strategic Analysis (2-3h, $10-15)—alternatives research, requirements validation, Phase 5 reconnaissance, strategic recommendation with user approval checkpoint. Phase 1: Foundation Validation (4-5h, $20-30)—fix gzip, measure entity preservation (hybrid: automated+spot checks+real-world), critical stress tests (prioritized), cost measurement, go/no-go. Phase 2: BigAGI Integration (4-8h, $20-40)—detailed design, IndexedDB schema, category selector, fill indicators, control buttons, message structure, proxy config, E2E testing. Phase 3: Production Hardening (2-3h, $10-15)—error handling, performance optimization, documentation. **Autonomy Architecture:** 4-tier file system. Tier 0: PROJECT_CHARTER, STRATEGIC_CONSTRAINTS (immutable). Tier 1: MASTER_PLAN, CURRENT_PHASE, PHASE_PLAN (frequent updates). Tier 2: DECISIONS_LOG, TEST_RESULTS, BUGS_FOUND, COST_TRACKING, ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS (append-only). Tier 3: PROGRESS_REPORT (hourly), INTERRUPT (user-created), ESCALATION (Claude-created). Tier 4: RECOVERY_CHECKPOINT (2h), CONTEXT_SUMMARY (on compression), CRITICAL_FACTS (immutable after creation). **Contingency Plans:** Entity preservation <99%: ≥99% PASS, 95-99% CONDITIONAL PASS (document), 90-95% investigate Agent 2 (2 iterations), <90% FAIL escalate. Critical bugs: 0 PASS, 1-2 attempt fixes, ≥3 STOP escalate. Phase 5 too complex: >8h escalate with alternatives, high breaking risk escalate recommend prototype. **Estimates:** Time 12-19h (Phase 0: 2-3h, Phase 1: 4-5h, Phase 2: 4-8h, Phase 3: 2-3h). Cost $60-100. Risks: HIGH (Phase 5 complexity, BigAGI breaking changes, entity preservation <99%), MEDIUM (multiple critical bugs, performance, integration complexity), LOW (cost overrun, time overrun, documentation quality).

# COMPRESSED CONTEXT ## Context Management Paradigm
Modern LLMs (Claude Opus 4.6) face Context Bloat: exponential compute costs, linear TTFT degradation, sharp reasoning drops. "Lost in the Middle": relevant data drowns in noise from outdated logs, failed calls, dead-end branches. BigAGI uses Active Context Compression: autonomous checkpoints, knowledge consolidation, physical pruning. Achieves 22.7% reduction (14.9M→11.5M tokens), maintains accuracy. Architecture: Forgetting Engine (two-agent compression) + Remembering Engine (file-based memory, Letta/MemGPT style). ## Forgetting Engine ### Agent 1 (Selector)
LLM-as-Judge evaluates information density per block. 75% of tool output = redundant. Selector finds low-negentropy zones (noise, error logs, intermediate reasoning), marks for compression. Uses "sawtooth" pattern: identifies blocks between subtask start/completion. Strict JSON output with line coordinates. Evaluation criteria:
- Dead-end explorations: debugging iterations, failed tests, discarded hypotheses
- Verbose tool outputs: raw logs, terminal dumps where only conclusion matters
- Conversational filler: long reasoning with conclusion established elsewhere NEVER select: dense specs, active code, formulas, API keys, already-compressed content. Constraints: max 5 blocks, each ≤10k tokens, non-contiguous allowed. Economic optimization: prefer larger blocks (fewer Agent 2 calls). Output JSON:
{ "blocks": [{ "start_line": 1000, "end_line": 1250, "estimated_tokens": 10000, "reasoning": "Why compressible" }], "total_tokens_to_free": 15000 }
``` ### Agent 2 (Compressor)
Tactical compression requires extreme precision - losing single variable breaks session. Chain of Density (CoD) research: iterative entity enrichment creates exponentially informative summaries at fixed length. Classic CoD generates sparse initial summary
